name: Chaperone and Heat-Shock Support Coverage Repair Assessment for June 10, 2026

assessment_window.basis: Coverage repair created after the June 10 surveillance assessment requested NPC open-label extension, current label, registry-boundary, review, and taxonomy repair before ordinary surveillance resumed.

summary: Coverage is now adequate for the current low-confidence, NPC-specific human_functional_benefit public claim, pending review and publication of the staged repair bundle. The repair stages PMID 40663813 for the NPC-002 48-month open-label extension, the March 2026 DailyMed Miplyffa label, an updated NPC study, two bounded findings, and a refreshed outlook. Coverage is not strong because the evidence remains concentrated in rare-disease NPC, the mammalian aging intervention base is absent, post-approval real-world data are not yet primary-source curated, and heterogeneous HSP/passive-heating/chaperone biology still needs periodic taxonomy discipline.

evidence_categories.rationale: The public/staged map now includes the randomized arimoclomol NPC phase 2/3 trial, the approval-era rescored NPC analysis, the 48-month NPC open-label extension, FDA approval context, the current DailyMed label, null ALS and inclusion body myositis trials, a short BGP-15 metabolic proof-of-concept trial, and a small hyperthermia HSP70 biomarker study.
evidence_categories.gap_note: This is enough for the current bounded NPC-specific stage, but it does not support broad aging benefit or generalized proteostasis restoration.

evidence_categories.rationale: No independently citable post-approval real-world Miplyffa or natural-history comparison source was staged. Sponsor and conference materials remain leads rather than primary public evidence.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Future surveillance should stage real-world or natural-history context only when primary, source-reliable data are available and materially change safety, durability, or uptake interpretation.

evidence_categories.rationale: The public baseline still includes HSF-1 C. elegans lifespan and aggregation biology but no curated mammalian lifespan, healthspan, or multimorbidity intervention evidence for chaperone or heat-shock support.
evidence_categories.gap_note: This category remains a true limitation on broad aging interpretation and is why coverage is not strong.

evidence_categories.rationale: Mechanism coverage includes HSF-1 worm biology, arimoclomol heat-shock-response amplification in NPC, BGP-15 HSP co-inducer context, and direct human HSP70 hyperthermia biomarker limits.
evidence_categories.gap_note: The staged outlook keeps arimoclomol, BGP-15, passive heat, HSP90, and generic chaperone biology separated rather than treating them as interchangeable evidence streams.

evidence_categories.rationale: The map captures two major arimoclomol null disease-program boundaries, the thin and indirect BGP-15 endpoint, the small hyperthermia HSP70 null biomarker result, and the current label's adverse-reaction, warning, and OCT2-interaction limits.

The staged repair adds the 48-month NPC open-label extension and the current DailyMed label to the existing FDA approval and null disease-program safety context. This repairs the named high-priority durability and label gap for the current public claim.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Adequate here means the public limitation is represented, not that chronic broad-use safety is established.

evidence_categories.rationale: The repair re-checked the main arimoclomol NPC, ALS, and IBM registries plus ALS and IBM open-label extensions, Gaucher boundary context, NPC early-access context, and the BGP-15 dose-ranging registry. Only NCT02612129 changed the staged public record because it anchors the NPC open-label extension and completion details.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Continue ordinary surveillance for posted results or publications from early-access, non-NPC, BGP-15, passive-heating, HSP70, HSF1, and HSP90 boundary records.

evidence_categories.rationale: PMID 41567854, PMID 39715913, and PMID 42007957 were screened. They were not staged because the primary OLE paper and DailyMed label directly repaired the high-priority public gaps.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Review literature remains useful context, but current public source completeness no longer depends on staging these review records.

evidence_categories.rationale: The public/staged map includes the pivotal NPC randomized trial, the approval-era rescored analysis, the 48-month NPC open-label extension, FDA and DailyMed regulatory anchors, major null ALS and IBM trials, classic HSF-1 C. elegans aging biology, BGP-15 human biomarker context, and hyperthermia HSP70 biomarker boundary evidence.

The staged outlook and session exclusions now separate NPC-specific arimoclomol evidence, non-NPC arimoclomol disease-program boundaries, BGP-15 metabolic biomarker context, passive-heating physiology, HSP90 imaging or oncology, and generic molecular chaperone biology.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Future taxonomy work may still split heat exposure, HSP co-induction, chaperone-mediated autophagy, mitochondrial UPR, and lysosomal NPC therapy more finely, but the current public track boundary is adequate.

search_log_summary: Coverage repair used NCBI PubMed ESummary/EFetch for the NPC-002 OLE and review/DDI context; DailyMed API and label XML for the current Miplyffa prescribing information; ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 for the main arimoclomol trials, extensions, early-access context, Gaucher boundary context, and BGP-15 registry; and local evidence-map comparison against the latest public outlook and the June 10 surveillance coverage assessment.
source_selection_notes: PMID 40663813 was staged because it directly repairs the missing NPC-002 48-month open-label extension gap.
The March 2026 DailyMed label was staged because it is the primary source for current indication, safety, and OCT2 interaction language.
PMID 41567854 was screened but excluded because the label is the primary drug-interaction source selected for public use.
PMID 39715913 and PMID 42007957 were screened but excluded as review context that did not add primary evidence.
Non-NPC arimoclomol extensions, Gaucher, NPC early-access, BGP-15 no-results, passive-heating, and HSP90 records were treated as boundary checks rather than staged public evidence in this repair.

known_gaps.description: The repaired map still lacks human interventional evidence that chaperone or heat-shock support improves aging-relevant function outside rare-disease NPC.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Do not strengthen the broad aging read without independent human trials in aging-relevant or proteostasis-relevant populations.

known_gaps.description: The map has classic C. elegans HSF-1 lifespan biology but no curated mammalian lifespan, healthspan, or multimorbidity intervention evidence for chaperone or heat-shock support.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Treat the broad aging interpretation as tentative until mammalian or human aging-relevant intervention evidence is found.

known_gaps.description: Post-approval real-world Miplyffa use and natural-history comparisons remain represented only as sponsor or conference leads, not staged primary public sources.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Watch for source-reliable real-world, registry, or pharmacovigilance publications that materially change safety, durability, uptake, or discontinuation interpretation.

known_gaps.description: Human target-engagement evidence for generic heat-shock or chaperone induction remains thin and protocol-dependent, with passive-heating and HSP biomarker records not clearly tied to durable tissue proteostasis.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Keep passive-heating and HSP biomarker records as boundary context unless a result-bearing intervention source links target engagement to aging-relevant function.

known_gaps.description: Arimoclomol NPC, BGP-15 metabolic biomarkers, passive heat exposure, HSP90 imaging or oncology, chaperone-mediated autophagy, mitochondrial UPR, and generic molecular chaperone biology can still be over-collapsed in searches.
known_gaps.suggested_action: During ordinary surveillance, continue explicit exclusion notes for HSP records that belong to adjacent proteostasis, lysosomal, mitochondrial, oncology, or physiology contexts.

next_coverage_action: Run source-fidelity, interpretation, and safety review for the staged coverage-repair bundle. If approved and published, return Chaperone and Heat-Shock Support to ordinary surveillance with watch on post-approval Miplyffa safety, primary real-world NPC data, non-NPC arimoclomol context, BGP-15 results, passive-heating/HSP target engagement, and mammalian aging-relevant intervention evidence.
